mission.title: Install and Configure Docker Desktop Kubernetes
mission.description: Docker Desktop for Mac provides a seamless local development environment for building and testing containerized applications using Docker and Kubernetes. It is ideal for developers who need a lightweight Kubernetes setup for local testing. However, it may not be suitable for production workloads due to resource limitations and lack of advanced features.

steps.title: Download Docker Desktop for Mac
steps.description:
This step installs Docker Desktop, which includes Kubernetes support.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

```

> This opens the Docker Desktop download page.

steps.title: Install Docker Desktop
steps.description:
Run the downloaded installer to set up Docker Desktop on your Mac.

```bash

open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

sudo installer -pkg /Volumes/Docker/Docker.pkg -target /

```

> Replace with the actual path if downloaded elsewhere.
> This installs Docker Desktop.

steps.title: Enable Kubernetes in Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step configures Docker Desktop to run a local Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

open /Applications/Docker.app

sleep 10 && open -a Docker --args --enable-kubernetes

```

> Launch Docker Desktop.
> Wait for Docker to start and enable Kubernetes.

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Installation
steps.description:
Check that Kubernetes is running correctly within Docker Desktop.

```bash

kubectl cluster-info

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command shows the Kubernetes cluster information.
> This command lists the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Docker Desktop
uninstall.description:
This will remove Docker Desktop and all associated Kubernetes resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

open /Applications/Docker.app

docker-compose down

rm -rf ~/Library/Containers/com.docker.docker

```

> Launch Docker Desktop.
> Stop all running containers.
> Remove Docker Desktop application data.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Docker Desktop
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of Docker Desktop and Kubernetes.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

sudo installer -pkg /Volumes/Docker/Docker.pkg -target /

```

> Download the latest version.
> Open the downloaded installer.
> Run the installer to upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes cluster is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to Docker Desktop.

**Fix:**
Increase CPU and memory allocation in Docker Desktop settings.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl command not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'brew install kubectl'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:6443: connect: connection refused
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes is not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure Kubernetes is enabled in Docker Desktop settings.

troubleshooting.title: Docker Desktop fails to start
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Conflicting applications or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Restart your Mac and ensure no conflicting applications are running.

versionNotes.changes: Improved Kubernetes integration and updated to Kubernetes v1.30.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'Kubernetes is running' when executing 'kubectl cluster-info'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Mac meets the minimum system requirements for Docker Desktop.
